steps.title: Distribute the negative sign through the parentheses
steps.content:
Start with $5x^3 - 3x + 8 - (9x^6 + 5x - 3)$. The negative sign applies to every term inside the parentheses:

$$5x^3 - 3x + 8 - 9x^6 - 5x + 3$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Group terms by degree:
  • $x^6$ terms: $-9x^6$
  • $x^3$ terms: $5x^3$
  • $x$ terms: $-3x - 5x = -8x$
  • Constants: $8 + 3 = 11$

Combining these gives:

$$-9x^6 + 5x^3 - 8x + 11$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
